title: Refactor dashboard test_app.py — parameterize and strengthen assertions
analysis: test_app.py has three problems: (1) TestMorphSwap has 5 near-identical methods that each assert `hx-swap="morph:innerHTML"` page-wide — this always passes trivially since ANY section having it is enough. The hx-get URL is the only differentiator. Fix: parameterize over the 5 partial URLs and use proximity-based slicing (find hx-get index, slice window, assert hx-swap within window) — the same pattern already used by TestHtmxTimeout (lines 555-588) and TestAriaLivePollingSections (lines 621-649). (2) TestIndex has 5 `test_get_root_htmx_*` methods (lines 84-102) that assert `hx-get="/partials/<url>"` presence — identical to what the strengthened TestMorphSwap parameterized test will check (it finds hx-get by index, which implicitly asserts presence). These are fully redundant and should be removed. (3) The page-wide `hx-swap` assertion is separately covered by TestIdiomorphExtension and test_no_plain_innerhtml_on_polling_sections, so removing it from individual TestMorphSwap methods loses nothing. A module-level PARTIAL_URLS tuple will be introduced for the parameterization; this also positions future refactors of TestHtmxTimeout and TestAriaLivePollingSections to reuse it (out of scope here).

prerequisites.description: Run existing test suite to establish a passing baseline: `cd dashboard && uv run pytest tests/test_app.py -v`

steps.description: Add PARTIAL_URLS module-level tuple (the 5 partial URL paths) and write a new parameterized test `test_section_uses_morph_swap` in TestMorphSwap. Use @pytest.mark.parametrize('partial_url', PARTIAL_URLS). Inside: find `hx-get="{partial_url}"` index in full HTML, slice a [-200:+200] window, assert `hx-swap="morph:innerHTML"` within the window. This provides a strictly stronger assertion than the page-wide check. Run tests — 5 new parameterized cases plus the 5 old methods should all pass (10 total in TestMorphSwap plus the no_plain_innerhtml test).

Remove the 5 individual test methods from TestMorphSwap: test_memory_section_uses_morph, test_recon_section_uses_morph, test_orchestrators_section_uses_morph, test_performance_section_uses_morph, test_memory_graphs_section_uses_morph. Keep test_no_plain_innerhtml_on_polling_sections. Run tests — TestMorphSwap should now have 6 tests (5 parameterized + 1 no_plain_innerhtml).

Remove the 5 redundant hx-get presence tests from TestIndex: test_get_root_htmx_memory, test_get_root_htmx_recon, test_get_root_htmx_orchestrators, test_get_root_htmx_performance, test_get_root_htmx_memory_graphs. These are now fully covered by TestMorphSwap's parameterized test (which calls html.index(hx-get=...) — implicitly asserting presence — and additionally verifies proximity of hx-swap). TestIndex retains: test_get_root_returns_200, test_get_root_content_type_html, test_get_root_contains_dark_factory, test_loading_skeletons_present. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Use a module-level PARTIAL_URLS tuple rather than defining it inside the test class
design_decisions.rationale: Multiple test classes (TestMorphSwap, and potentially TestHtmxTimeout, TestAriaLivePollingSections in a future refactor) share the same set of 5 partial URLs. A module-level constant avoids duplication and makes it a single source of truth. Tuple is used over list since these are immutable test fixtures.

design_decisions.decision: Window size of [-200:+200] around the hx-get index for proximity-based slicing
design_decisions.rationale: TestHtmxTimeout uses [-200:+500] and TestAriaLivePollingSections uses [-100:+300]. For hx-swap proximity, the attribute is typically on the same HTML element as hx-get, so a symmetric [-200:+200] window is sufficient. This matches the closest precedent while being conservative enough to avoid false positives from adjacent sections.

design_decisions.decision: Remove TestIndex hx-get tests entirely rather than parameterizing them
design_decisions.rationale: The strengthened TestMorphSwap test implicitly asserts hx-get presence (html.index() raises ValueError if not found) and additionally verifies hx-swap proximity. Keeping a separate parameterized TestIndex test for the same hx-get strings would be pure duplication with no added coverage.

This test serves a different purpose — it's a negative assertion ensuring no section uses plain innerHTML swap (which would break morphing). It's not redundant with the positive proximity assertion and doesn't benefit from parameterization.

reuse.where: TestHtmxTimeout (lines 555-588) and TestAriaLivePollingSections (lines 621-649) in the same file
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: html.index('hx-get="..."') → slice window → assert attribute within window. This is the established convention for section-specific HTML assertions in this test file.
Continue using the same `client` fixture that creates a TestClient for the FastAPI app — no new fixtures needed.

reuse.where: Standard pytest API, not yet used in this file but idiomatic
reuse.how: Import pytest (already available), use @pytest.mark.parametrize decorator with the PARTIAL_URLS tuple.
